threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: When you ask for a bus schedule and the driver just looks at you like you’ve summoned a dinosaur. It’s all about the apps now, mate.
Caught a 196 last night; two guys were arguing over who saw the craziest thing on the bus. One swore a woman was knitting a whole blanket!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids tagging the old S-Bahn station while the sun set, I realized this place breathes art even amidst the chaos.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find spontaneous techno parties in abandoned buildings, with locals and tourists dancing under flickering strobe lights until dawn.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by how they know to avoid Civic Center during BART chaos. Tourists are still looking at their maps while we just find another way.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5 last week, and it stopped because a guy decided to do yoga on the sidewalk. The driver just shook his head and waited.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the Festes de Gràcia are just for selfies, but locals spend all year crafting those decorations. It’s a real labor of love.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by how often they mispronounce 'Gràcia' as 'Gracia'—that sharp 'ç' is a rite of passage here.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the buses run on a schedule instead of following a vague suggestion of when they might show up.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded the whole bus with old school Reggae. Never thought I'd see randoms get up and dance at 2 AM in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a street artist painting a mural while kids played soccer next to an open-air bar. Only in Neukölln do those worlds blend so effortlessly.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Kreuzberg's late-night kebab shops serve currywurst-flavored wraps that are bizarrely addictive. You won't find that combo anywhere else!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they navigate the BART delays. If they sigh and pull out a book, they get it; tourists just look lost.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tux on the 5-Fulton this morning, looking like he just left a wedding—only in SF would that be a casual commute.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always miss the fact that locals rarely sit down at the plaça. We’re too busy standing at the bar, sipping our vermut and chatting with friends.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their reluctance to order a 'torta de patata' at the local bakery. Only locals know it’s a must-try.
